_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket index, which averages prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice relative to 2015 (base = 100), stands at 132, indicating an approximate 32% increase since 2015. This rise is not uniform across all items and has occurred unevenly over the period. The index captures global commodity price movements for common breakfast staples as tracked by financial databases like FRED and IMF. Its recent climb reflects distinctive trends affecting individual commodities rather than a broad-based price inflation.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged the most, increasing by 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice up 22%, wheat by 7%, and sugar only 5%. These price changes are largely driven by weather events and crop diseases such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice, and frost and drought in arabica coffee-growing regions. These soft commodities trade in relatively thin markets, meaning limited supply disruptions can cause significant price volatility rather than reflecting generalized inflation pressures.
notes.why: A small set of weather-sensitive crops primarily explain the overall rise in this breakfast basket. Because these commodities depend heavily on climatic factors, their prices may remain elevated for extended periods due to persistent supply constraints and recurring agricultural challenges.
